content: Just as the pedagogical definition of reflexion frames it as a deliberate metacognitive step for human language learners to correct output and align meaning with intent, LLM self-reflection mechanisms can be redesigned to embed this structured, human learning-style reflexion scaffolded by the linguistic definition of the term, rather than the ad-hoc iterative refinement most current systems use. This re-framing treats LLM self-reflection not just as an AI technical trick, but as a structured second-language acquisition-style practice for LLMs learning to generate aligned, correct output.
